title: Malty Flaky Indian Lamb Korma
description: A rich and comforting lamb curry from the Indian subcontinent, featuring a malty, flaky texture from the addition of beer and a blend of warming spices. This recipe is halal-friendly and can be adapted to suit a variety of diets.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the ginger and garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the lamb and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the cinnamon, cardamom, cayenne, turmeric, coriander, and cumin, and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the beer, yogurt, water, and butter, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven. Braise the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender and easily shredded with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the chopped cilantro. Season the korma with salt to taste.
Transfer the korma to a serving dish and serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Serve with basmati rice or naan bread, if desired.
